‎The Notebook is a romantic drama released in 2004. It is based on the novel by Nicholas Sparks. It tells the emotional story of Noah Calhoun and Allie Hamilton, two young lovers from different social worlds who are torn apart by circumstance but remain deeply connected across time. Set mainly in the 1940s, the film explores themes of true love, sacrifice, memory, and destiny. ‎ ‎Their relationship faces numerous obstacles, yet their bond proves powerful and enduring. ‎ ‎Framed through an elderly man reading their story to a woman in a nursing home, the film shows how love can survive even through illness, aging, and the passage of years ‎ 🎵 Lloyd Vaan - Present 🎥 The Notebook (2004) #hopecore #mindset #mentality #thenotebook

Can’t believe this video of Audrey faking sleep had over 10 million views. Always wondered if I was bias but I guess it’s me and other 10 million people all thought the same thing: Duh! Audrey is cute!!!! 🤣🥰 Most people thought it’s a cute video, and that it’s relatable, but with great viewership, comes with some unbelievable hate and negativity. Some people thought I was shaking her too hard, some thought I’m not supposed to wake her to feed, some told me to get her checked out because she might have medical condition, some are so sure that she’s actually asleep and I’m a stupid f**k for thinking she’s faking it, and this is apparently why parents abuse their children because they are pinning this behavior on children when they aren’t even capable of. I’m honestly at awe of how one little innocent video could have this much hate. While I appreciate the concern from some of the commenters, I honestly feel bad for those who had such anger issue and really thought that I was abusing my child by forcing her to eat or calling her fake. I hope that someday, their trauma is healed. 🫣 #viralbabyvideos #viralbaby #babydrama #childactress #cutestbabyaward

You sent an attachment.
After 20hrs of unmedicated labor, Our baby boy joined us 1/14/2026 4:07am 7lbs 3oz Kyle never left my side from the moment we left the house, to laying in the hospital bed with me as I gave my last push. Providing counter pressure, encouragement, and being my advocate. In his words “Team Melanie”. Ziggy Myles DeSousa🤍 Yes, that is his real name. No, it is not short for anything. A name we are proud of and a name he will get to define on his own. Myles is a mash up of Mel & Kyle, a little piece of us he will carry with him, always. That was the hardest thing I have ever done, and the greatest pain I have ever experienced. My water don’t break until 9.5cm, when my incredible midwife kindly broke it for me. Nothing could have prepared me for labor, and I over plan for everything. Hours of research, learning, listening to those more experienced than me, breath work prep…none of it could truly capture what it would be like. It was a wild ride. I’m so grateful for a healthy delivery. A wonderfully tough, but empowering experience. Holy moly women are truly amazing! I believe labor rewired something in my brain. The world seems softer. My heart feels steady and not so restless. I’m taking these recovering days seriously, slowly & fully jumping into motherhood. #pregnancy #labor #birthannouncement #baby #motherhood
melschoonover

You sent an attachment.
😍 Interesting fact: Atlantis: The Lost Empire (2001) — To ensure the “subterranean” world felt authentic, the production team pivoted from Disney’s traditional soft curves to a high-energy, angular aesthetic inspired by Hellboy creator Mike Mignola. This was a massive technical hurdle, as the crew had to develop custom “shading” software to blend 3D CGI vehicles with hand-drawn characters—a hybrid style that was years ahead of its time. They even hired a professional linguist to create a functional Atlantean language, making it the first Disney film to feature a fully unique, speakable dialect! The movie almost had a different vibe, as it was originally pitched as a musical. Fortunately, the creators pivoted to a “pulp-adventure” epic, even hiring a real-life “geology consultant” to help design the glowing, crystal-powered technology of the 2026 25th Anniversary 4K restoration.

🎬 The Bachelor 1999 ‧ Comedy/Romance ‧ 1h 41m 📜 Storyline Jimmie Shannon is a commitment‑phobic guy enjoying his carefree life until his girlfriend Anne catches the bouquet at a friend’s wedding. When he finally tries to propose, he bungles it badly and she walks away. Soon after, Jimmie learns his recently deceased grandfather left him a $100 million inheritance and control of the family business — but there’s a catch. He must be married by 6:05 p.m. on his 30th birthday the very next day to claim it. Facing a ticking clock, Jimmie races through the city tracking down old girlfriends and scrambling for love, only to rediscover what matters most along the way. 🎞️ Title Info 💍 The Bachelor: A romantic comedy about love, deadlines, and unexpected self‑discovery. 📅 Release date: November 5, 1999 (USA) 🎬 Director: Gary Sinyor ✍️ Writer: Steve Cohen 🎭 Main cast: Chris O’Donnell, Renée Zellweger, Hal Holbrook, James Cromwell, Artie Lange, Edward Asner, Marley Shelton, Sarah Silverman, Brooke Shields, Mariah Carey 🏢 Distributed by: New Line Cinema 📏 Running time: ~1 hour 41 minutes ⭐ IMDb rating: 5.1/10 🍅 Rotten Tomatoes: ~9% critics score 📝 Rated: PG‑13 💰 Budget: $21 million 💵 Box office: ~$36.9 million worldwide 🎖️ Notes: Remake of the Buster Keaton silent comedy Seven Chances with modern twists and ensemble comedy antics. 📺 Where to Watch You can stream The Bachelor on Netflix with a subscription in many regions. It’s also available to rent or buy on digital platforms like Amazon Prime Video, Apple TV, and other online movie stores depending on your country. 🔔 Follow @ekazop for more content like this! 💌 DM for credit or removal requests (no copyright infringement intended for video, photo, or music). You sent an attachment.
HOW OBSERVING PARENTAL AFFECTION POSITIVELY WIRES A BABY’S BRAIN: Secure Attachment: When your baby sees you and your partner hugging, smiling, or speaking gently, it reinforces a sense of safety. Visible warmth between caregivers signals stability and dependability. Over time, this helps your baby build trust, confidence, and healthier future relationships. Nervous System Regulation: Babies co-regulate before they self-regulate. Observing calm affection softens their stress response. Tone, facial expression, and touch communicate safety, wiring their nervous system toward stability rather than hypervigilance — even when the affection is not directed at them. Modeling Healthy Love: From infancy, children form subconscious blueprints for relationships. Witnessing warmth, laughter, and mutual respect teaches them that love is safe and cooperative, shaping how they later show affection and navigate conflict. Emotional Intelligence Development: Babies constantly study emotional cues. Seeing affectionate interactions helps them connect facial expressions with positive feelings, strengthening empathy and early social understanding. Long-Term Emotional Security: Openly expressed love between caregivers creates a stable emotional climate. Children raised in warm environments often develop greater resilience, lower anxiety, and stronger self-worth. Everyday Affection Matters: Love does not need to be dramatic. Small, consistent gestures — sitting close, brief hugs, kind words — quietly build the emotional foundation that supports lifelong psychological security. // by: Ainsworth, Mary D. S., Blehar, M. C., Waters, E., & Wall, S. (1978). Patterns of Attachment: A Psychological Study. Hillsdale, NJ: Lawrence Erlbaum.

The video is a brief, emotionally dynamic compilation of infant and toddler reactions, capturing moments of distress, curiosity, and sudden joy. It opens with a crying baby in a striped shirt, whose tears cease as a pacifier is gently placed in their mouth at 00:10. Moments later, a baby in a white onesie reaches out with wide-eyed curiosity toward a black spherical object, suggesting fascination. A toddler in a white shirt is seen crying while an adult’s hand rests on their face, likely attempting to soothe them. This is followed by a baby in a black hoodie, initially crying but quickly transitioning to laughter as an adult pats their head, highlighting a shift from sadness to delight. The final scene shows a baby in a white and gray shirt holding the same black spherical object, displaying a look of surprise or confusion, as if processing a new experience. The entire sequence, spanning 24 seconds, presents a microcosm of early emotional development—marked by vulnerability, exploration, and the comforting influence of adult presence—rendered through intimate, candid moments. #BabyLaughter #FunnyCats #CuteMoments #BabyJoy #ChildhoodMemories

To cast The Wolt ot Wall Street (2013), Martin Scorsese specifically sought a fresh face who possessed the raw power to go toe-to-toe with Leonardo DiCaprio. Casting director Ellen Lewis championed Robbie early in the process, preferring her unknown status over established names like Blake Lively and Olivia Wilde. Capitalizina on this desire for a fresh dvnamic, Robbie won the role by fully committing to the high-maintenance "Duchess of Bav Ridge" persona. She secured her place by adopting a thick Brooklvn accent during the chemistry read that convinced the team she was the only one capable of grounding the film's chaos.. #usareels #canada #america #i̇ran #african

1. We rushed our relationship way faster than we should’ve. In high school we moved too fast and skipped steps that would’ve helped us grow the right way. 2. We became too dependent on each other. When you love someone it’s easy to want them 24/7, but you still have to grow individually and build your own identity. 3. We let other people’s opinions leak into our relationship. When you’re young everyone thinks they know what’s best for you, but what matters is what actually works for your relationship. #couple #love #relationships #engagment #fiance

You sent an attachment.
The real magic of The Notebook is not the letters or the rain or the years in between. It’s the way Ryan Gosling and Rachel McAdams look at each other. Their chemistry feels unpolished, almost accidental. Like two people constantly on the edge of laughing or arguing or falling apart. You believe the fights. You believe the tenderness. You believe that these two could spend a lifetime hurting each other and still choose to stay. That kind of connection cannot be written into a script. It either exists in the space between two actors, or it does not. In The Notebook, it does. #rachelmcadams #ryangoslingedit #thenotebookmovie #romcoms #arijitsingh

Visit Puerto Vallarta’s Taqueria for a Unique Taco Experience!🦝🌮 💡At a unique taqueria in Puerto Vallarta, raccoons roam the dining area, stealing food and sitting at tables with customers while they eat. The taqueria has embraced these playful creatures, making them part of the authentic and quirky experience. Known for its delicious tacos and relaxed atmosphere, this spot offers a dining experience like no other. The raccoons add an extra layer of fun, making it a must-visit for those looking for a memorable meal surrounded by local wildlife. Khloe sent an attachment.
After spending seven months in hibernation, Chada, a Himalayan brown bear living in a sanctuary in Ukraine, was captured on camera slowly emerging from her den. The footage shows the careful, almost uncertain steps of an animal re entering the world after a long winter sleep. Hibernation allows bears to conserve energy during harsh months, slowing their metabolism and living off stored fat. Waking up is not sudden. It is a gradual return to movement, balance, and awareness.
